channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports

item.title: Fox News Digital Sports' college football winners and losers: Week 12
item.description: There were plenty of eye-opening plays in Week 12 of the college football season. But who won and lost Saturday? Take a look at the latest interation of winners and losers.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Chargers beat Bengals 34-27 on Dobbins' late TD after squandering 21-point lead
item.description: INGLEWOOD, Calif. (AP) — Jim Harbaugh and the Los Angeles Chargers finally have their signature win. Even if it ended up being a white-knuckle finish. The Chargers squandered a 21-point third-quarter lead Sunday night against the Cincinnati Bengals before J.K.
item.dc:creator: JOE REEDY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Jazz comeback falls short against Clippers, 116-105
item.description: LOS ANGELES (ABC4 Sports) - James Harden moved into second place on the NBA's career 3-point list and had 20 points and 11 assists to help the Los Angeles Clippers beat the Utah Jazz 116-105 on Sunday night to end a three-game losing streak. Harden moved past Ray Allen with No.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Backup QB Arbuckle leads Toronto past Winnipeg 41-24 for the Argonauts' 19th Grey Cup title
item.description: VANCOUVER, British Columbia (AP) — Backup quarterback Nick Arbuckle led the Toronto Argonauts to their record-extending 19th Grey Cup title, throwing two second-half touchdown passes in a 41-24 victory over the Winnipeg Blue Bombers on Sunday night at B.C. Place.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Chargers stave off Bengals' comeback in epic Sunday night thriller
item.description: The Los Angeles Chargers' J.K. Dobbins scored the game-winning touchdown with just seconds left in the fourth quarter to defeat the Cincinnati Bengals.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: James Harden breaks tie with Ray Allen, moves to No. 2 on NBA's career 3-point list
item.description: INGLEWOOD, Calif. (AP) — James Harden now stands alone at No. 2 in 3-pointers. The Los Angeles Clippers guard connected on the 2,974th 3-pointer of his career Sunday night and broke a tie with Basketball Hall of Famer Ray Allen for the No. 2 spot on the NBA’s list.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: When Nick Bosa left with another oblique injury, Seahawks’ offense awakened and took down the 49ers
item.description: Nick Bosa's injury definitely hurt the 49ers in loss to Seahawks and there's concern he could miss additional time.
item.dc:creator: Jerry McDonald, The Mercury News

item.title: Darius Garland scores 25, Cavaliers become 4th team to start 15-0 with 128-114 win over Hornets
item.description: Darius Garland scored 25 points, Ty Jerome matched his career-high with 24 in place of Donovan Mitchell and the Cleveland Cavaliers stayed unbeaten with a 128-114 win over the Charlotte Hornets.
item.dc:creator: Tom Withers, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: NBA fines Bucks coach Doc Rivers for comments about call refs admitted they got wrong
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Doc Rivers was right. It still cost him $25,000. Rivers, the coach of the Milwaukee Bucks, was fined by the NBA on Sunday, a day after he publicly criticized the officiating that led to the deciding points in a 115-114 loss to the Charlotte Hornets.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: The Dallas Wings win the WNBA draft lottery, earning the No. 1 pick next year
item.description: The future of the Dallas Wings got a lot brighter as the franchise won the WNBA draft lottery on Sunday and with it the chance to draft UConn star Paige Bueckers with the No. 1 pick. “We know what No.
item.dc:creator: DOUG FEINBERG, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: What the 49ers said after losing to the Seahawks
item.description: Fred Warner's take on the 49ers' blown leads this season: "It's not like us, but that's what we've shown this year so I guess until we stop doing that, then that's what we are.
item.dc:creator: Michael Nowels, The Mercury News

item.title: Josh Allen seals Bills' 30-21 win over Chiefs with TD run, ending bid for perfect season
item.description: Josh Allen ran for a 26-yard touchdown on fourth down with 2:17 left, sealing the Buffalo Bills’ 30-21 win over the Kansas City Chiefs and handing the two-time defending Super Bowl champions their first loss of the season.
item.dc:creator: John Wawrow, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: 49ers fall after late TD pushes Seahawks past San Francisco 20-17
item.description: SANTA CLARA, Calif. (AP) — Geno Smith scored on a 13-yard scramble with 12 seconds remaining and the Seattle Seahawks snapped a six-game losing streak against the San Francisco 49ers with a 20-17 victory on Sunday.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Josh Allen, Bills hand Chiefs 1st defeat in nearly a year
item.description: Josh Allen and the Buffalo Bills have handed Patrick Mahomes and the Kansas City Chiefs its first loss since Christmas Day 2023, as there is no longer an undefeated team in the NFL.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: NBA fines Hornets star LaMelo Ball $100K for anti-gay comment during postgame interview
item.description: Charlotte Hornets do-it-all point guard LaMelo Ball was fined $100,000 for making an anti-gay comment during his postgame interview after beating the Milwaukee Bucks.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: CMU drops final game at home against Western Colorado
item.description: GRAND JUNCTION, Colo. (KREX)—After a six-game winning streak, Colorado Mesa University was dealt a setback by South Dakota Mines. Now, the Mavericks return to their home field with the stakes as high as the Rocky Mountains, aiming to secure a playoff spot.
item.dc:creator: Titus Cleveland, KREX 5

item.title: Utes get run over by #17 Colorado, 49-24
item.description: BOULDER, Colo. (ABC4 Sports) - The Utes losing streak continues, with no end in sight. Shedeur Sanders overcame a shaky start to throw three touchdown passes, LaJohntay Wester returned a punt 76 yards for a score and No. 18 Colorado pulled away late to beat Utah 49-24 on Saturday.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Jake Paul, 27, beats 58-year-old Mike Tyson as the hits don't match the hype
item.description: ARLINGTON, Texas (AP) — The boos from a crowd wanting more action were growing again when Jake Paul dropped his gloves before the final bell and bowed toward 58-year-old Mike Tyson.
item.dc:creator: SCHUYLER DIXON, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Thousands of Netflix users report outages during Tyson-Paul boxing match
item.description: Just as thousands of Netflix users flocked to the app to stream Friday night's head-to-head boxing match between champion Mike Tyson and YouTuber-turned-boxer Jake Paul, the stream crashed.
item.dc:creator: Russell Falcon, WGN 9

item.title: Bird, Fowles, Pondexter, Beard headline Women's Basketball Hall of Fame Class of 2025
item.description: WNBA greats Sue Bird, Sylvia Fowles, Cappie Pondexter and Alana Beard headline the Women's Basketball Hall of Fame Class of 2025 that was announced Friday.
item.dc:creator: DOUG FEINBERG, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Goalkeeper Olivia Bodmer leads Rutgers back to NCAA tournament
item.description: NEW JERSEY (PIX11) – Rutgers women's soccer will host UConn in the opening round of the NCAA tournament on Saturday. This is the Scarlet Knights’ 13th consecutive appearance in the tournament, but how they got there was something Rutgers nation had never seen before.
item.dc:creator: Perry Sook, PIX 11

item.title: VIDEO: Mike Tyson slapped Jake Paul at their final weigh-in. Here's why Tyson claims he did it
item.description: A meeting between Mike Tyson and Jake Paul briefly devolved into chaos on Thursday after Tyson slapped Paul across the face during the final weigh-in before their highly anticipated fight on Friday.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, Fox 5

item.title: Blackhawks lose 3-1 in Seattle
item.description: SEATTLE (AP) — Jaden Schwartz scored on a power play 1:34 into the third period, and the Seattle Kraken pushed their winning streak to three games with a 3-1 win over the Chicago Blackhawks Thursday night. Matty Beniers and Brandon Tanev also scored.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Andrei Vasilevskiy approaching 300th NHL career win
item.description: A win against the Winnipeg Jets (15-1-0) would break a four-game losing skid for the Tampa Bay Lightning (7-6-1) and make history for one special player.
item.dc:creator: Aileen Hnatiuk, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Chicago Sports Network launching subscription streaming service
item.description: CHICAGO — The Chicago Sports Network is launching a streaming service Friday amid its negotiations with Xfinity. The subscription service will start at $19.99 for one team or $29.99 for full access to CHSN. A 7-day free trial is being offered.
item.dc:creator: Andy Koval, WGN 9

item.title: 18-year-old arrested for throwing water bottle at football game between U of U, BYU
item.description: An 18-year-old was arrested after reportedly throwing a water bottle onto the field during the BYU-Utah game on Nov. 9.
item.dc:creator: Trevor Myers, ABC 4

item.title: Chicago Sky introduce new head coach Tyler Marsh
item.description: CHICAGO (WGN) — A little more than a month after former head coach Teresa Weatherspoon was unceremoniously relieved of her duties, the Chicago Sky introduced their new head coach to the Windy City Tuesday. Tyler Marsh, who was hired on Nov.
item.dc:creator: Eli Ong, WGN 9

item.title: Dodger Stadium improvements could provide leg-up in team's pursuit of World Series repeat
item.description: Work has begun at Dodger Stadium, including renovations to the home team clubhouse, which houses team lockers, showers and communal areas.
item.dc:creator: Travis Schlepp, NewsNation

item.title: Report: Some Bears veterans request Bagent start over Williams
item.description: CHICAGO — A new report Tuesday sheds some light on the firing of Bears offensive coordinator Shane Waldron. Marc Silverman of ESPN 1000's "Waddle and Silvy" reported Tuesday morning that players went to Matt Eberflus and Ryan Poles to request to remove Waldron. It doesn't end there.
item.dc:creator: Andy Koval, WGN 9
